own_review_points: MappingSettings @settingDefault says TemporalTableStrategy.SINGLE_TABLE, but TemporalTableStrategy.AUTO javadoc states 'This is the default' — doc is wrong
TemporalHelper.createHistoryTablePrimaryKey handles only RootClass and Collection with no else — any other Temporalized type silently gets no history-table PK
InFlightMetadataCollectorImpl.addTable: needless cosmetic ternary flip (name!=null?x:null -> name==null?null:x), pure diff churn

Human: with nullable row_end the 'as of' query needs an OR predicate (row_end>=t or row_end is null) which forces a BitmapOr plan; using a max-timestamp sentinel for active rows enables a direct index scan (significant perf impact)
notes: Huge PR (159 files, +10620) truncated at ~150k. Verified good: the @settingDefault SINGLE_TABLE-vs-AUTO doc mismatch (TemporalTableStrategy.AUTO explicitly 'This is the default'), the 'SUpplier' typo, and the missing else in createHistoryTablePrimaryKey. WRONG: P3 mischaracterizes OracleTemporalTableSupport.useTemporalRestriction — v8 invents a 'nested ternary' with a 'transaction ID supplier' branch that 'delegates to parent', but the visible code is a simple switch (HISTORY_TABLE -> getTemporalIdentifier()!=null; NATIVE -> false; default -> true), no supplier, no parent delegation. P7 (Temporalized.java) and P8 (UpdateCoordinatorHistory.java) cite files past the truncation marker -> unverifiable. P4 (currentTransactionIdentifier mutable state) is a weak, self-defeating thread-safety + doc nit -> trivial; P6 (cosmetic ternary) trivial. Missed the human's meaty nullable-row_end vs max-timestamp query-plan performance argument.
